summary: A per-user, cosmetic, disposable UI preference is client-side browser state — never governed configuration and never code.

rules.rule: Never persist a per-user, cosmetic, disposable UI preference in the governed configuration/settings store or in code — it persists in the browser (cookie or equivalent).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before persisting any value, apply the boundary test — 'if this value were wrong for the next person, would anyone need to audit, administer, or explain it?' — routing yes to governed config and no to client-side preference.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never place secrets, tokens, tenant identifiers, or any governed config value in the client preference store.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Read the preference post-hydration, never at SSR render — the server renders with the system default (a code constant) and the client synchronizes from the cookie in the first effect after mount; only the override is persisted.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Use a brand- and product-neutral key/prefix namespace for the client preference store — the name reflects the kind of value (a UI preference), not the system or vendor that hosts it.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never persist a value that fails the boundary test client-side as a shortcut to avoid building an admin surface.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
